commentPreTextItems.html: Reju: "Never understood why there would not be a 3rd man in test matches. Too many runs are leaked in that direction through edges which is really demotivating and frustrating for bowlers." The bowlers want the edges to go to slip and not over or away from them. So starting with a fielder at deep third will make it a slightly defensive move

commentTextItems.html: Defended from the crease against the length ball
Fuller and outside off, pushed back to the bowler
Shortish length around middle and off, defends it towards gully from the crease

commentPreTextItems.html: Lavish Bhateja: "Told earlier that Akshadeep is taking atleast 2 wickets before lunch but you did not publish that. Now the way he is bowling, i feel he should be the third seamer in the squad after Bumrah and Shami in Australia. Thoughts?" He is putting in a strong performance to stake that claim for sure

commentTextItems.html: Wide outside off, slanting away on a length, left alone
Length ball on middle and leg, defended towards midwicket
Pulls his length back to land it on a fullish length, slanting away, he pokes at it and narrowly misses edging it

commentPreTextItems.html: A bit of a field change now. Two slips and two gullies

commentTextItems.html: Fuller and slanting away, gets forward and plays it under his eye, towards cover
Another four via an outside edge. Siraj angles it away from off on a length, Shanto presses forward and gets it through slips and gully

commentPreTextItems.html: Abhi: "Both the DRS calls that India have taken in this inning look "weird" on TV. The successful one looked like it was missing leg and the current one to Shanto appeared to have pitched inline and heading for the stump. I don't know if this is the effect of ageing eyesight or if the DRS on TV looks off!" The Deep lbw call was mainly because of the lowish bounce Green Park is known for. The Siraj appeal was a pure error in judgement. So nothing off with DRS

commentTextItems.html: Outside off on a length, kept out towards point

commentTextItems.html: Slanting away well outside off, he leaves it be, 135.8kph

Celebrappeal from Siraj but the umpire does not think so. Pant seems to have convinced Rohit for the DRS. This one lands on a length and shapes back in a touch. Shanto hops to flick it away but is beaten on the inside edge and is hit on the flap of the pad. Did it land in line? Nope, pitched outside leg. India lose a review
Soft hands gets Shanto his third four in six balls. Lunges forward to defend the length ball with a wobble seam that is angled across. Gets an outside edge that goes between gully and the cordon
Fuller on off, Mominul taps it in front of cover and sprints to get off the mark on his 15th ball

dismissalText.commentary: Zakir Hasan c Jaiswal b Akash Deep 0 (24b 0x4 0x6) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: an excellent catch by Jaiswal and Deep has struck in his first over. The umpires, though, want to check the fairness of the catch. It was a length ball outside off. Zakir came forward to defend. Hard hands and gets a thick outside edge that goes low to the right of Jaiswal at slip. He goes with both hands but replays are too blurry to judge if the ball bounced before he caught it. Finally, there is one angle from which the TV umpire concludes that it went straight into the hands. And he also says there is no angle which shows the ball bounced. So Zakir departs for a 24-ball duck

dismissalText.commentary: Shadman Islam lbw b Akash Deep 24 (36b 4x4 0x6) SR: 66.66

dismissalComment.html: a huge, huge appeal for lbw, especially from the bowler. Rohit has gone for the review after a bit of discussion. Was a length ball that came in with the angle. Shadman was on his toes as he looked to flick it. Got beaten and the ball-tracking shows it hit him slightly down the middle stump and would have gone on to hit a good chunk of leg stump. Bangladesh lose their second wicket. From the first replay it seemed umpire's call at best but when the projection came up, even Rohit couldn't believe it

dismissalComment.html: an excellent catch by Jaiswal and Deep has struck in his first over. The umpires, though, want to check the fairness of the catch. It was a length ball outside off. Zakir came forward to defend. Hard hands and gets a thick outside edge that goes low to the right of Jaiswal at slip. He goes with both hands but replays are too blurry to judge if the ball bounced before he caught it. Finally, there is one angle from which the TV umpire concludes that it went straight into the hands. And he also says there is no angle which shows the ball bounced. So Zakir departs for a 24-ball duck

dismissalComment.html: a huge, huge appeal for lbw, especially from the bowler. Rohit has gone for the review after a bit of discussion. Was a length ball that came in with the angle. Shadman was on his toes as he looked to flick it. Got beaten and the ball-tracking shows it hit him slightly down the middle stump and would have gone on to hit a good chunk of leg stump. Bangladesh lose their second wicket. From the first replay it seemed umpire's call at best but when the projection came up, even Rohit couldn't believe it
